chunks.title: Chipotle Oil Inquiry
chunks.summary: The speaker investigates what oils Chipotle uses in their food preparation. They discover that rice bran oil is used for rice, sunflower oil for chicken and peppers, and canola oil in tortillas.
chunks.transcript: does Chipotle use seed oils well I'm here at Chipotle I'm going to find out let's go see how's it going I was just curious which oils you cook things in like so the rice we use rice brand oil rice brand oil and the rice chicken and we use sunflower oil with sunflower and then is also sunflow oil sunflower oil okay this whole row sunflow oil rice rice BR oil okay cool and then in the in the tortilla there's canola oil in the tortilla okay okay cool all right so

chunks.title: Concerns About Canola Oil
chunks.summary: The speaker expresses concerns about the ingredients in a Chipotle burrito, especially highlighting the use of canola oil. Issues with canola oil such as its linoleic acid content and its association with health problems are discussed.
chunks.transcript: I'll do a steak burrito let's do white rice P beans I'll skip the beans no thank you you want to do peppers any mild hot sauce on uh sure let's do mild okay any sour cream or cheese uh you can do cheese so that on the burrito that's it that's fine yeah thank you all right guys so listen I ate a lot of Chipotle when I was growing up in terms of fast food this is not the worst thing I've ever heard of but as you guys heard when I was ordering let's break it down and just so you know I'm going to give this burrito to somebody else I'm not going to eat this burrito we've got a steak burrito here from Chipotle it's not entirely good by my opinion the tortilla on the outside is made from gluten obviously not a fan of gluten probably going to damage your gut create inflammation cause leaky gut there's also canola oil in the tortilla canola oil is a seed oil about 25% linolic acid and there's a lot of problems with canola oil that I've talked about in the past specifically it has a little bit of fatty acid called arusc acid in it there's a type of oil connected with canola oil called rape seed oil but there's so much arusc acid in it and arusc acid causes heart lesions it causes cardiac myol lipidosis in rats so in Canada they made canola oil canola is an acronym for Canadian Oil low acid they genetically modified R seed plants to be lower in arusc acid so normal R seed oil is about 40% aric acid canola oil is about 2% of rusic acid but I still have concerns that you're getting

chunks.title: Problems with Seed Oils
chunks.summary: The dangers of seed oils are examined, including their potential to cause metabolic dysfunction and heart issues. The speaker discusses the high levels of linoleic acid in these oils and its effects on human health.

chunks.title: Debunking Common Food Misconceptions
chunks.summary: The speaker addresses misconceptions about rice types, stating that white rice is preferable to brown rice in terms of arsenic content. They emphasize that both rice and seed oils are generally not ideal dietary choices.
chunks.transcript: specifically contain this amount of these organic solvents but Benzene was in perer at 700 times less levels and it was pulled off the shelves so just one problem with seed oils that you can see in the production these organic solvents that are used to clean the oils to extract the oils from the gums of the seeds end up in the oils then the oils are bleached deodorized sodium hydroxide all kinds of things that heated to very high temperatures I think 500° causing massive amounts of damage and oxidation of these oils even before you get them or they end up in your food go ahead do me a favor if you hit that like And subscribe button right now it helps me continue to make videos like this that are free that are educational that help you make better decisions for your life to improve your health to educate your friends and your family and live better longer lives hopefully you guys enjoy so we got rice the rice is cooked in rice brand oil that's a seed oil significant amounts of lenic acid I believe around 40% lenic acid in rice brand oil interestingly though I'm not going to eat this burrito I opted for white rice versus is brown rice if you guys are torn between white rice and brown rice remember don't cook it in rice brand oil and know that brown rice is going to have a lot more Arsenic and heavy metals than white rice I've done experiments with white rice in my diet I don't really love it it's a grain I'm not a huge fan of this often moldy but if you want a carbohydrate Source white rice is probably the least bad of the grains it's better than wheat better than oats in my opinion but again I'm not a huge

chunks.title: Cumulative Effects of Seed Oils
chunks.summary: The speaker summarizes how the accumulation of linoleic acid from seed oils in the body can lead to various health issues, including cardiovascular disease. They argue for the avoidance of seed oils in favor of animal fats.

chunks.title: Call to Action for Chipotle
chunks.summary: In the conclusion, the speaker calls on Chipotle to eliminate seed oils from their menu and suggests alternative cooking fats, such as butter or tallow, which were historically used and deemed healthier.
chunks.transcript: seed oils things that humans would never have had evolutionarily they're new to our diets over the last 100 years before that we were eating exclusively animal fats that's what you should be eating I'm calling you out Chipotle you should get rid of seed oils for your foods I think cooking in butter and Tallow would be the best but even olive oil would probably be a better cooking fat for your steak for your peppers and for your rice then rice brain oil and sunflower oil so that's my take on Chipotle guys I'm going to go find somebody outside to give this burrito to see you in the next
